w:r.w:t: The Step-by-Step Shopify Store Migration Guide (
Thinking about moving your store to Shopify? Good instinct. But the process feels overwhelming, right?
Customers unable to see their order history
With the right approach, you can migrate in just
and avoid all of these problems.
This guide walks you through it step-by-step. No fluff, no jargon. Just actionable steps you can take today.
Do You Actually Need to Migrate to
Before you start, let's confirm Shopify is right for you.
You should migrate if you're currently on:
Any platform that's holding you back
You DON'T need to migrate if:
You're already on Shopify or Shopify Plus
Your business is very small (<10 products, <$1k/month revenue)
You're happy with your current platform and it's growing
2: What You Need to Know (Before You Start)
5 Things Every Merchant Should Know About Shopify Migration
2-7 days with the right tools. Here's the breakdown:
automates 90% of the work. Without it, you're manually transferring data (which takes forever).
Most merchants who do it manually end up taking 2-3 months because they get stuck on details.
Let's be real about the cost:
Option A: DIY with a Tool
Shift It Now: Free or $200-500 depending on store size
Option B: DIY + Some Professional Help
Use a tool + get 10-20 hours of professional support. You get most of the benefits at 30% of the cost.
No, if you do it right. Here's what you need to do:
Set up 301 redirects (old URL → new URL)
With these steps, you preserve 90-95% of your rankings. Rankings may dip for 2-4 weeks, then recover.
Yes, you'll lose rankings. Don't do that.
4. Will Your Customers Lose Access to Their
Shopify doesn't import passwords (for security). So yes, customers need to reset their passwords.
Send an email: "Reset your password here: [link]"
Some customers will ignore it (that's normal)
For security-conscious merchants: This is actually a feature, not a bug. Password reset ensures old passwords aren't exposed.
You've backed up your original data, right?
Never delete your old store until 30 days after going live.
You can go back to your old store (have a backup)
Rerun the migration to fix issues
Customers can still access their old accounts
This is why automation tools are worth it—they can re-run the migration without losing data.
Grab a piece of paper (or spreadsheet) and answer these:
Do you have any custom code? (Yes/No)
If you have 500+ products or custom integrations, hire professional help. DIY gets complicated.
Medium-sized stores who want peace of mind
Go with Option B. You get the speed of a tool + the peace of mind of professional support.
Pick a date 2-4 weeks from now. Why?
Gives you time to prepare without rushing
Avoids busy sales periods (don't migrate during Black Friday!)
customers adjust to the new store
Tuesday-Thursday. Avoid Fridays (if something breaks, no support). Avoid Mondays (people are busy).
How to Migrate to Shopify (The Simple
Okay, here's where it gets real. The actual migration happens in 7 steps.
Step 1: Set Up Your Shopify Store
Go to Shopify.com and click "Start free trial"
Enter your email and create a password
You now have an empty Shopify store
Customization, branding, or settings yet. We're moving fast.
Install Shift It Now (or your tool of choice)
Connect your old store (give it access to your product data)
Choose what to import (products, images, descriptions, pricing)
Export products from old store as CSV
Reformat the CSV to match Shopify's template
Upload to Shopify admin > Products > Import
Always test with 10 products first. Don't import everything at once.
If you want customers to see their past orders and account history:
Export customers from old store (email, name, address)
Import via CSV to Shopify admin > Customers > Import
Send customers an email: "Reset your password here"
Passwords don't transfer. Customers must reset them.
Write a blog post titled "How to Preserve Customer Data During Ecommerce Migration" — agencies and forums will link to this.
This is critical. Your domain is your brand. Do it right.
Option A: Point Domain to Shopify
Go to your domain registrar (GoDaddy, Namecheap, etc.)
Update DNS settings to point to Shopify
In Shopify admin > Settings > Domains > Add existing domain
Option B: Transfer Domain to Shopify
Shopify can handle your domain directly
Do this step last (right before launch). Once you point your domain, your old store goes offline.
Every old URL needs to redirect to the new URL:
In Shopify admin > Settings > URL Redirects
Add all your old URLs and where they should redirect
Test 5-10 redirects to make sure they work
Google sees the redirects and preserves your rankings
Prioritize product pages and popular blog posts. Don't worry about every single URL.
Before you go live, test everything:
Invite 2-3 trusted customers to test before launch. Get real-world feedback.
The moment of truth. Here's what happens:
Send email to customers: "Heads up, we're moving to a new platform tomorrow!"
Point your domain to Shopify (the switch happens)
Monitor the store for 30 minutes (check for errors)
Respond to customer support emails quickly
Monitor for broken links or 404 errors
Check Google Search Console for crawl errors
30 minutes of active work | then monitor for 24 hours
How to Keep Your SEO Rankings After Migration
This is where most merchants mess up. Don't be that person.
Take a screenshot of your top 20 keywords
Save this somewhere (you'll compare it later)
you can verify your rankings recover after migration.
Every old URL must redirect to a new URL. Google sees the redirect and passes ranking authority from the old page to the new page.
Redirect ALL product pages (these are usually your money pages)
Redirect less important pages (if there's time)
Check that old URLs redirect to new ones
Monitor rankings in Google Search Console
Expect a temporary dip (2-4 weeks is normal)
Check rankings for your top 20 keywords
Compare to the screenshot you took before migration
If rankings haven't recovered, investigate why
Most rankings recover in 2-4 weeks if you set up redirects correctly.
Should You Use a Migration Tool? (Honest
60 hours of your time × $50/hour = $3,000 cost. A migration tool costs $500. Using a tool saves you $2,500 and gives you peace of mind.
Zero downtime (migrate while store is live)
Free or $200-500 depending on store size
Start with Shift It Now (free tier). If you need advanced features, upgrade. If you need help, they offer consultation.
The 5 Biggest Migration Mistakes (And How to Avoid
Mistake #1: Not Backing Up Your Original Data
You delete your old store after migration. Then something breaks. You panic.
Export everything from your old store before migration (products, customers, orders, emails)
Keep backups in two places (external drive + cloud)
Wait 30-60 days before deleting the old store
Keep the export files forever (you never know when you'll need them)
You migrate on a Friday afternoon without testing. Something breaks. No support available. You lose a weekend of sales.
You lose all your SEO rankings. Traffic disappears. Revenue drops.
Set up redirects BEFORE pointing your domain
Monitor Google Search Console after launch
If you see 404s, fix them immediately
Customers show up and the store looks different. They get confused. Support tickets flood in.
Email customers 1 week before: "We're moving to a new platform"
Email customers day of: "Here's what changed"
Email customers day 1 after: "We're here if you have questions"
Pin an announcement on your homepage
Mistake #5: Not Testing the Payment System
You go live. Customers try to buy. Payment processing is broken. You lose sales.
Test a real transaction in staging first (use a test credit card)
Check that order confirmation emails send
Monitor payments for the first 24 hours
Migration is done. But the work isn't over. Here's what to do:
Monitor Google Search Console for errors
Check Google Search Console daily (fix any crawl errors)
Check if rankings have started recovering
Review customer feedback (what do they like/dislike?)
Set up new Shopify features (abandoned cart, email marketing, etc.)
Ready to Migrate? Here's Your Next Step
If you've read this far, you know:
Migration is doable (2-7 days with a tool)
It doesn't have to be expensive ($200-500)
You won't lose rankings (with proper setup)
There are tools to automate the hard parts
Audit your current store (products, customers, data)
Install Shift It Now (free tier available)
We're here if you get stuck
Start Free with Shift It Now →
Automated migration in days, not weeks. Free to install, pay only if you use it.
2-7 days with a tool. 2-4 weeks manually.
$200-500 for a tool. $1,700-3,500 with professional help. $3,000-15,000+ for full agency service.
No, if you set up 301 redirects. Expect a
Passwords don't transfer. Customers reset via email link.
You've backed up everything, right? You can go back to your old store.
WooCommerce, BigCommerce, Magento, Squarespace, 3dcart, Etsy, and others.
